purpose: Bind the read-only STM, P, and SG demand-coordinate inputs, their evidence vocabulary, and their promotion firewall before Round 03 can claim capability coverage or a residual gap.

bindingSource.role: Binds the four consumer-research locators and the read-only capability-survey boundary; it is a navigation aid rather than repository or standards authority.

sources.role: Research-refresh intake, source identity, axis-separation decision, evidence corrections, and non-promotion boundary.
sources.authority: read-only research input; not a standard, taxonomy acceptance, Skill specification, or implementation authorization

sources.role: Claim, source, directness, strength, authority, applicability, verification, adoption, freshness, and promotion vocabulary.
sources.authority: read-only corrected evidence ledger; not a standard, final corpus, Skill admission, or implementation authorization

sources.role: Candidate STM and historical P coordinate identities, axis distinction, case-record shape, and split/merge lifecycle rules.
sources.authority: M1 audit structure only; content and placement remain candidates and authorize no Skill or baseline change

sources.role: Candidate SG identities, P/STM crosswalk, owner distinctions, under-owned areas, evidence lanes, and promotion firewall.
sources.authority: diagnostic routing matrix only; not an accepted standard, Skill specification, repository-role mutation, or implementation plan

bindingRules: STM, P, and SG are distinct many-to-many axes; none replaces another and their historical counts are not completion targets.
A coordinate identifies a research or control-demand location; it does not prove recurrence, importance, capability insufficiency, a residual gap, or the need for a Skill.
Every future demand record must cite coordinate IDs, source IDs, evidence claim IDs when applicable, evidence state, uncertainty, held claims, affected subjects, and recheck triggers.
Held, rejected, superseded, locator-only, contradicted, version-mismatched, or unsupported evidence cannot support a residual-gap claim.
Consumer-research bodies remain read-only external inputs and are not vendored, copied into release payload, or treated as durable standards custody by this repository.
promotionFirewall: Research synthesis, source prestige, recency, taxonomy membership, under-ownership, or an existing first-party draft cannot directly authorize a Skill, Hook, project standard, runtime control, or repository mutation.

A later gap decision requires bounded demand evidence, native and runtime baseline evidence, external and composed alternative comparison, material residual failure, counterevidence, control-cost analysis, accountable ownership, and verification.
A later standard candidate remains non-authoritative until separately delivered to and adjudicated by YIYUAN-CALIBRATION and any applicable project or domain authority.
Hook consideration remains downstream of supported residual-gap evidence and host-specific suitability; the default remains no Hook.
recheckTriggers: a source hash, version, stage, adoption state, or authority boundary changes
STM, P, or SG is split, merged, renamed, superseded, or remapped
held source gaps or material counterevidence change a demand coordinate
a host, model, loader, permission, workspace, Skill, Hook, or runtime capability materially changes
a downstream gap, carrier, standard-candidate, or cross-Agent claim is proposed

allowed: bind immutable source identity and research authority state
use coordinate IDs as read-only survey inputs after owner activation
derive bounded demand records that preserve evidence and uncertainty
blocked: copying the external research bodies into this repository
treating a coordinate or under-owned area as residual-gap proof
promoting a standard, authoring or adapting a Skill, enabling a Hook, mutating runtime, or writing another repository
activating Round 03 discovery or authorizing remote push

verificationSurface: the handoff and four source identities are immutable and complete
the STM, P, and SG family ranges, counts, sources, axes, and authority states are explicit
evidence vocabulary matches the corrected ledger without flattening independent dimensions
binding rules reject direct coordinate-to-gap and coordinate-to-Skill inference
promotion, Hook, standards-custody, redistribution, runtime, cross-repository, and discovery boundaries fail closed
repository verification and mutation tests reject source, family, evidence-state, or authority drift
nextGate: governed Round 03 demand records and dated native/runtime baseline before public candidate metadata discovery
